$description: FieldOps Motion Semantic Layer — named motion intents aliased to primitives. Engineers consume these tokens, never the primitives directly. Naming grammar: {what-happens}.{duration|easing}. Field principle: if in doubt, use a shorter duration. Operators' perception speed under task pressure compresses subjective time — a 300 ms animation feels like 500 ms on the bus.

duration.$description: Semantic duration aliases. Map to primitive duration scale.

instant.$description: Reduced-motion target. Also: focus-ring appearance, skip-link reveal. Duration is literally 0 — no easing applied.

micro.$description: Tap feedback, toggle snap, checkbox fill. The sensation of hardware response.

fast.$description: Hover states, badge color swap, icon swap within a button.

respond.$description: The default interactive response. Button press, input focus ring, status pill change.

transition.$description: Small motion: tooltip appear, dropdown open, accordion expand (short content).

enter.$description: Component entering the screen: sheet slides in, AlertBanner mounts, modal opens.

layout.$description: Layout-level changes: step advancement in GuidedStep, card reordering, list re-sort.

narrative.$description: Celebratory / onboarding only. Completion confetti, first-launch flow. Never in task flow.

easing.$description: Semantic easing aliases — use these names in component specs.

default.$description: Fallback for any transition without a specific intent.

enter.$description: Something arriving. Sheet, panel, modal, toast.

exit.$description: Something leaving. Always pair with a shorter duration than its enter.

confirm.$description: Success confirmation: step checkmark, button success state. One overshoot only.

settle.$description: Organic settle without drama: panel slides, sheet entries. No overshoot.

appear.$description: Badge/chip appears in place (scale from 0). Lightweight pop.

alert.$description: System-level state change: connectivity flip, critical error. Blunt — no charm.

progress.$description: Progress bars, loaders, spinner rotation. Linear only — easing on progress feels broken.

reduced-motion.$description: Rules for @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce). Not a token set — these are implementation rules.

strategy.$description: Do not simply remove motion. Substitute: fade opacity-only transitions (no translate/scale) at duration.instant. Connectivity indicators switch to color-only with no animation.

allowed.$description: The one animation class always permitted even under reduced-motion. Opacity change at instant or micro duration is imperceptible as motion but preserves temporal information.

forbidden.$value: translate, scale, rotate, spring-snappy, spring-gentle, bounce-subtle
forbidden.$description: All spatial and spring-based animations are disabled.
